growth_records.cite: Delaby et al. Phenotypic plasticity in cell elongation among closely related bacterial species. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-60005-y. [PMC12130487]
growth_records.snippet: For dual short-pulse FDAA labeling in A. excentricus... The cells were grown in PYE at 26 °C for 5% of their doubling time (6 min).
growth_records.notes: Doubling time derived: pulse duration stated as '5% of their doubling time (6 min)', so doubling_time = 6/0.05 = 120 min = 2.0 h. mu = ln2/2.0 = 0.347 h^-1. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 2295 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/2295

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
